summary_en: CMK Corporation revised its dividend policy from a 30% payout ratio to a 3% DOE (dividend on equity) framework, raising FY2027 guidance from ¥20 to ¥28 per share.
title_en: Notice of Change in Dividend Policy and Revision of Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase)
translation_en_brief: On 19 May 2026, CMK Corporation announced a revision of its dividend policy and an increase in dividend guidance for fiscal year ending March 2027. The company is transitioning from a traditional payout-ratio framework (targeting approximately 30% consolidated payout ratio) to a new metric: DOE (Dividend on Equity, defined as annual dividend divided by consolidated equity excluding non-controlling interests and other comprehensive income). The new target DOE is 3%, which the company believes will enhance dividend predictability and optimize shareholder capital levels. For FY2027, the annual ordinary dividend per share is being raised from the previously forecast ¥20 to ¥28 per share, representing an increase of ¥8. No interim (second quarter) dividend is planned. This policy shift reflects management's commitment to strengthening shareholder returns while maintaining necessary internal reserves for business development and strengthening the company's financial position.
